tagged_story_for_tts: <[silence:3400ms]>Brain rewiring itself. <[silence:1300ms]> Skills shape its structure.<[silence:1300ms]>"Neuroplasticity" means your brain’s ability to reorganize—like clay reshaping after each touch.<[silence:700ms]>Every thought, habit, or injury tweaks its wiring.<[silence:1300ms]>London taxi drivers’ brains prove it.<[silence:700ms]>Memorizing 25,000 streets grows their hippocampus—the memory center—like a muscle after weightlifting.<[silence:700ms]>Scan their brains, and the region bulges visibly.<[silence:1300ms]>Stroke victims relearn speech by rerouting signals around dead zones.<[silence:700ms]>Practice a violin for years?<[silence:700ms]>The finger-controlling cortex expands, stealing space from less-used areas.<[silence:700ms]>Even blindness sharpens other senses—hearing and touch hijack unused visual real estate.<[silence:1300ms]>But plasticity cuts both ways.<[silence:700ms]>Chronic stress shrinks the prefrontal cortex (your "brake pedal" for impulses).<[silence:700ms]>Addiction carves deep grooves—like a river eroding rock—making habits harder to escape.<[silence:1300ms]>Three related subjects are memory formation, habit loops and brain injuries.<[silence:1200ms]>
clean_story: Brain rewiring itself.  Skills shape its structure. "Neuroplasticity" means your brain’s ability to reorganize—like clay reshaping after each touch. Every thought, habit, or injury tweaks its wiring. London taxi drivers’ brains prove it. Memorizing 25,000 streets grows their hippocampus—the memory center—like a muscle after weightlifting. Scan their brains, and the region bulges visibly. Stroke victims relearn speech by rerouting signals around dead zones. Practice a violin for years?The finger-controlling cortex expands, stealing space from less-used areas. Even blindness sharpens other senses—hearing and touch hijack unused visual real estate. But plasticity cuts both ways. Chronic stress shrinks the prefrontal cortex (your "brake pedal" for impulses). Addiction carves deep grooves—like a river eroding rock—making habits harder to escape. Three related subjects are memory formation, habit loops and brain injuries.
